# Questions in Livestream 66

Live Stream #66: Osiris, Vampires, and Other Undead Playas.

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6MHlQAmAbSI?t=418" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">6:58</a> | Why were ancient polytheistic systems so willing to synchronize and conflate deities with very different personalities and myths? | He said loose polytheistic metaphysics, especially in systems like Gnosticism, made it easy to allegorize other religions and absorb their texts, figures, and symbols. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6MHlQAmAbSI?t=834" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">13:54</a> | Does Judges 19:1 imply that Judges was written later than the events it describes? | Yes. He said phrases like `in those days` and `when there was no king in Israel` point to later retrospective writing, probably from the monarchy period. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6MHlQAmAbSI?t=1453" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">24:13</a> | Are the Gihon and Pishon the Nile and the Indus, or entirely different rivers? | He said they are different rivers and argued all four Eden rivers belong in a Mesopotamian setting, with the `Gihon` tied to `Kish`, not African Cush. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6MHlQAmAbSI?t=1768" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">29:28</a> | Which Bronze Age site now being excavated in Israel is most important? | He favored Jericho over Hazor because further Late Bronze evidence there could radically reshape the conquest debate. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6MHlQAmAbSI?t=2201" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">36:41</a> | What is the most biblical position on baptism and salvation? | He argued baptism does not itself save, but it is a necessary act of obedience and can be described as an invitation into the salvation process. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6MHlQAmAbSI?t=2737" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">45:37</a> | How should 2 Kings 3:27 and the sudden Israelite withdrawal after Mesha's sacrifice be understood? | He read it as a coalition that partly broke apart, leaving Israel exposed; in that setting Moab was able to reverse the outcome without implying Chemosh overpowered Yahweh. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6MHlQAmAbSI?t=3100" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">51:40</a> | Could Daniel 9:25 be referring to a decree from Artaxerxes II rather than Cyrus or Artaxerxes I? | He said Artaxerxes II is too late and treated the restoration decrees as belonging to the earlier Persian sequence centered on Cyrus, Darius, and Artaxerxes I. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6MHlQAmAbSI?t=3595" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">59:55</a> | What is going on in Genesis 6:1-4, and does it connect to Egypt? | He read the passage mainly through ancient Near Eastern cultic categories rather than angelic-human breeding, with stronger Mesopotamian than Egyptian parallels. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6MHlQAmAbSI?t=4066" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:07:46</a> | Were Samuel and Kings originally one continuous book? | He said 1-2 Samuel were one book and 1-2 Kings were one book, but whether Samuel and Kings were originally a single larger work remains uncertain. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6MHlQAmAbSI?t=4368" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:12:48</a> | Was the Memphis cult at its height under Ramesses II, and does that help the late-date or Mosaic-authorship case for Genesis 1? | He said the cult reached its fullest expression much later, especially in Dynasty 25, though it was already old and influential enough to belong to the larger background of Genesis 1. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6MHlQAmAbSI?t=4661" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:17:41</a> | Does he have book recommendations on parapsychology or supernatural phenomena? | He said he had posted a serious bibliography on this topic before but could not recall it offhand, and said he would repost it when he found it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6MHlQAmAbSI?t=4836" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:20:36</a> | Is the Great Pyramid's King's Chamber the same size as the Holy of Holies? | He said no: the King's Chamber is rectangular rather than square, so the supposed size match does not hold and would not mean much even if it did. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6MHlQAmAbSI?t=5338" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:28:58</a> | Does he recommend a scholarly New Testament chronology for studying Jesus' ministry? | He did not offer a specific chronology title, but pointed viewers toward the Gordon-Conwell Center for the Study of Early Christianity bibliography as a strong starting point. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6MHlQAmAbSI?t=5644" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:34:04</a> | What should be made of the alleged early Jerusalem curse inscription publicized by Gershon Galil? | He was strongly skeptical, saying it looked more like overread marks than a secure inscription and that the claim may expose wider problems in related epigraphic arguments. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6MHlQAmAbSI?t=6106" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:41:46</a> | Was the tribe of Dan largely a mixed Sea Peoples group, making Samson more like a Greek hero than a good Israelite judge? | He said Dan certainly became compromised and disobedient, but he would not explain that mainly by Philistine mixing; in his view Dan was already on a bad trajectory. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6MHlQAmAbSI?t=6461" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:47:41</a> | Is a reconstructed timeline placing Moses' birth, flight, Exodus, and death in the late 14th to early 13th century BC reasonable? | He said yes, calling that broad chronology a good fit with the archaeological and historical framework he uses. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6MHlQAmAbSI?t=6693" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:51:33</a> | Does 1 Chronicles 29:20 justify worshiping God's agents because the people bowed to both God and David? | No. He distinguished respect or reverence from worship and said the text does not authorize worship of apostles, Mary, prophets, or any other human agent. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6MHlQAmAbSI?t=7320" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:02:00</a> | Are we close to archaeology indisputably proving the Bible's historicity, or are we already there? | He said we are already there in the limited historical sense that archaeology confirms many people, places, and events, even though it cannot directly prove miracles. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6MHlQAmAbSI?t=7730" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:08:50</a> | What real difference is there between God knowing every detail of all time and God being present at every place and time? | He distinguished omniscience from omnipresence, arguing that God's knowledge does not require pantheistic-style presence in all places. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6MHlQAmAbSI?t=8225" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:17:05</a> | How can Judges 17:7 call a man both `of the family of Judah` and a Levite? | He said the verse refers to clan and tribe at different levels: a Levite can belong to a Judah-named clan without belonging to the tribe of Judah. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6MHlQAmAbSI?t=8613" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:23:33</a> | In Genesis 10:6 and 10:13-14, are names like Mizraim and the related groups actual Egyptian individuals or clans? | He treated many of them as personified peoples or national designations rather than recoverable individual Egyptians from genealogical records. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6MHlQAmAbSI?t=8993" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:29:53</a> | Is `griffin` a plausible translation for `cherub`? | No. He said cherub imagery changes over time, but `griffin` is not a good translation or even a very good comparator. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6MHlQAmAbSI?t=9284" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:34:44</a> | Was Nimrod known in the ancient world under another name? | He said we do not know, and he was unwilling to treat popular identifications such as Sargon of Akkad as secure. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6MHlQAmAbSI?t=9445" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:37:25</a> | Are there good source recommendations for studying slavery in the Old Testament? | He pointed generally toward Richard Averbeck and possibly Michael Brown, while stressing that the field moves quickly and is not his main specialization. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6MHlQAmAbSI?t=9747" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:42:27</a> | Which flood text is older, Genesis or Gilgamesh? | He said Gilgamesh is probably older in written form, but that does not by itself prove Genesis copied it, since the parallels are much thinner than often claimed. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6MHlQAmAbSI?t=9965" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:46:05</a> | Is open theism, middle knowledge, or Molinism a potential salvation issue? | He said middle knowledge can be used in orthodox Molinism, but open theism is outside orthodoxy because it leaves God's future knowledge and promises uncertain. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6MHlQAmAbSI?t=10157" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:49:17</a> | What kind of evidence would be needed to move the Ramesside dating of the Exodus? | He said it would take much more than challenges to the name `Ramesses`, because the date also rests on multiple toponyms, synchronisms, and broader settlement evidence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6MHlQAmAbSI?t=10586" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:56:26</a> | Is the Bible's concept of sin and ritual cleanliness unique? | He said ritual impurity has parallels in Egypt, but the Bible develops a more fully moralized concept of sin than Egypt shows until very late periods. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6MHlQAmAbSI?t=11089" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:04:49</a> | Does Hezekiah's seal use Egyptian solar imagery and ankhs? | Yes. He said the seal carries Egyptian symbolism, but that does not mean Hezekiah was worshiping Egyptian deities. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6MHlQAmAbSI?t=11278" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:07:58</a> | Does Cain's fear that others might kill him imply humans besides Adam and Eve? | He said it implies a wider descended population from Adam and Eve, not a contradiction of the text or a separate human creation line. |
